ProjectTitle: 8801 Corkscrew Road Structure Demolition - RFB 2023-04
Summary: 8801 Corkscrew Road Structure Demolition - RFB 2023-04. Action: Approved award of Request for Bids No. RFB 2023-04, 8801 Corkscrew Road Structure Demolition to Southwest Builders Inc. to demolish the structure located at 8801 Corkscrew Road at a grand total cost of $106,890, approve a contingency fund amount of $10,689 (an amount equal to 10% of the total project cost) to cover unforeseen circumstances which may occur, and authorize the Village Manager to execute the contract documents on beh...
ActionTaken: Approved award of Request for Bids No. RFB 2023-04, 8801 Corkscrew Road Structure Demolition to Southwest Builders Inc. to demolish the structure located at 8801 Corkscrew Road at a grand total cost of $106,890, approve a contingency fund amount of $10,689 (an amount equal to 10% of the total project cost) to cover unforeseen circumstances which may occur, and authorize the Village Manager to execute the contract documents on behalf of the Village of Estero Council with the instructions that the Village Manager is not to move forward with the demolition until further direction from Council. Additionally, Resolution No. 2023-02 was clarified to correct the date of execution from March 15, 2023, to February 15, 2023 due to error
Outcome: Approved award of Request for Bids No. RFB 2023-04, 8801 Corkscrew Road Structure Demolition to Southwest Builders Inc. to demolish the structure located at 8801 Corkscrew Road at a grand total cost of $106,890, approve a contingency fund amount of $10,689 (an amount equal to 10% of the total project cost) to cover unforeseen circumstances which may occur, and authorize the Village Manager to execute the contract documents on behalf of the Village of Estero Council with the instructions that the Village Manager is not to move forward with the demolition until further direction from Council. Additionally, Resolution No. 2023-02 was clarified to correct the date of execution from March 15, 2023, to February 15, 2023 due to error
MotionText: Approved award of Request for Bids No. RFB 2023-04, 8801 Corkscrew Road Structure Demolition to Southwest Builders Inc. to demolish the structure located at 8801 Corkscrew Road at a grand total cost of $106,890, approve a contingency fund amount of $10,689 (an amount equal to 10% of the total project cost) to cover unforeseen circumstances which may occur, and authorize the Village Manager to execute the contract documents on behalf of the Village of Estero Council with the instructions that the Village Manager is not to move forward with the demolition until further direction from Council. Additionally, Resolution No. 2023-02 was clarified to correct the date of execution from March 15, 2023, to February 15, 2023 due to error

Summary: Woodfield Estero Planned Development - Rezoning (DCI2022-E005) (District 6) Vacant parcels at the northwest corner of US 41 and Coconut Road. Applicant seeks to rezone ±45.6 acres from Mixed Use Planned Development (MPD) to Estero Planned Development (EPD. Action: Recommended approval to the Council with staff conditions and the following additional conditions: the hard scaping and lighting will be consistent throughout the community and will be defined at the time of the first Development Or...
ActionTaken: Recommended approval to the Council with staff conditions and the following additional conditions: the hard scaping and lighting will be consistent throughout the community and will be defined at the time of the first Development Order, no 24-hour medical facilities use. The Village Council would recommend a minimum percentage of use during operating hours for the civic structure to be qualified as a civic structure, perimeter buffers along U.S. 41 and adjacent to the pond at the time of the first Development Order, shielding the fence in the buffer from the adjacent community, architecture review in more details at the time of Development Order, foundation plantings for the residential structures must exceed the minimum 10%, and the intersection of Coconut Road and Walden will be analyzed and the recommendation will be whatever the infrastructure that provides the highest and best capacity use for that intersection
Outcome: Recommended approval to the Council with staff conditions and the following additional conditions: the hard scaping and lighting will be consistent throughout the community and will be defined at the time of the first Development Order, no 24-hour medical facilities use. The Village Council would recommend a minimum percentage of use during operating hours for the civic structure to be qualified as a civic structure, perimeter buffers along U.S. 41 and adjacent to the pond at the time of the first Development Order, shielding the fence in the buffer from the adjacent community, architecture review in more details at the time of Development Order, foundation plantings for the residential structures must exceed the minimum 10%, and the intersection of Coconut Road and Walden will be analyzed and the recommendation will be whatever the infrastructure that provides the highest and best capacity use for that intersection

Summary: Fee Reduction as an Incentive in Village Center. Action: No formal action recorded. Fee Reduction as an Incentive in Village Center Village Manager Sarkozy stated that this agenda item was a result of the March 15, 2017 Council workshop related to the request of the Genova developer. At the workshop, two proposed options were discussed: building permit fee reduction or waiver for indoor (under building) parking and fee reduction or waiver for using a private provider company for building insp...
ActionTaken: No formal action recorded. Fee Reduction as an Incentive in Village Center Village Manager Sarkozy stated that this agenda item was a result of the March 15, 2017 Council workshop related to the request of the Genova developer. At the workshop, two proposed options were discussed: building permit fee reduction or waiver for indoor (under building) parking and fee reduction or waiver for using a private provider company for building inspections. Community Development Director Gibbs stated that the fees are set in the fee schedule, which did not include a provision for waivers. She also spoke to the options provided to Council in the Agenda Item Summary Sheet. Director Gibbs noted that parking garages required detailed inspections. She inquired whether fee reductions would be automatic for any project in the Village Center. Questions or Comments by: Councilmembers Levitan, Errington, Batos, Vice Mayor Ribble and May
Outcome: No formal action recorded. ActionTaken: No formal action recorded. Discussion: Location ofTwo New School Facilities in Estero Staff Presentation/Comments: Village Manager Sarkozy. Presentation/Comments: Chris Patricca, District 3 representative on the School District ofLee County School Board. The purpose ofthe discussion was to determine whether there was consensus among Councilmembers on where the School District should locate their two new facilities. Options considered were: (1) locating adjacent to the Estero High School site on Williams Road; and, (2) building on a Three Oaks Parkway 70-acre site. Council Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Boesch, Batos, Levitan, Wilson, McLain, Vice Mayor Errington and Mayor Ribble. Consensus ofthe Council indicated four in favor ofthe Three Oaks Parkway site and three in favor ofthe Williams Road location. Public Comment: Ed Weil, Rookery Pointe John Russell, Estero Palms The workshop session went into recess

Community Development Director Gibbs addressed the Comprehensive Plan transmittal process; she believed that the Village may have a response from the state next week. Presentation by: Norman Trebilcock, Trebilcock Consulting Solutions, PA, provided details of the approximate 3 Yz mile study area, which examined existing and future conditions; traffic counts collected during the peak season 2016; trips added from future developments directly impacting Coconut Road in Bonita Springs, unincorporated Lee County and the Village of Estero; and analyzed the intersection of US 41 and Coconut Road in terms of delay based on vehicle turning movement counts. ActionTaken: No formal action recorded. Estero Parkway Village Manager Sarkozy introduced the topic and spoke regarding the public comment process. Kyle Coleman, Assistant to Village Manager, offered a PowerPoint presentation including information regarding the initial step taken by providing a link to a survey on the Village website that ran for 10 days; 282 responses via online form, 15 responses via email, and 1 phone call; five optional road layouts were provided, as well as 2 preferred landscape density options, along with priority options for safety, beautification/ landscaping, cost, and speed. Mr. Coleman also spoke to the takeaways of the first step; significant community input; residents seem undecided on road layout; clear landscape density preference; greater survey details needed; and two phase vs. one phase decision needed for next steps. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - May 31, 2017 Page 3 o
Outcome: No formal action recorded. Estero Parkway Village Manager Sarkozy introduced the topic and spoke regarding the public comment process. Kyle Coleman, Assistant to Village Manager, offered a PowerPoint presentation including information regarding the initial step taken by providing a link to a survey on the Village website that ran for 10 days; 282 responses via online form, 15 responses via email, and 1 phone call; five optional road layouts were provided, as well as 2 preferred landscape density options, along with priority options for safety, beautification/ landscaping, cost, and speed. Mr. Coleman also spoke to the takeaways of the first step; significant community input; residents seem undecided on road layout; clear landscape density preference; greater survey details needed; and two phase vs. one phase decision needed for next steps.
